title: heracleum sphondylium subsp. carpaticum
intro: Heracleum sphondylium subsp. carpaticum, also known as Carpathian hogweed, is a subspecies of the common hogweed plant (Heracleum sphondylium) native to the Carpathian Mountains region. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in folk medicine, to treat various health issues. The plant's roots, leaves, and seeds are valued for their anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic properties, which are said to help alleviate conditions such as arthritis, fever, and urinary tract infections. Herbal preparations of Carpathian hogweed can be made into teas, infusions, or decoctions, often combined with other herbs, to harness its health benefits and medicinal properties.
